text.title: Spring Boot JUnit Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real Spring Boot test suites. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Controller test passes with no real code exercised: both `UserService` and `UserRepository` are mocked; the controller's only real work is reading mocked output.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent over-stubbed dependencies in a `@WebMvcTest` slice.
antipattern.fix: In a `@WebMvcTest` slice mock only the direct collaborator (the service). To test service-repository interaction, use a separate service-layer test class with `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`.

antipattern.symptom: `InvalidUseOfMatchersException: Misplaced or misused argument matcher detected here` on a `verify(svc).save(user, any())` call.
antipattern.root-cause: Mockito requires all arguments to be matchers OR all to be literals — mixing is illegal.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the literal with `eq(value)` when any other arg uses a matcher: `verify(svc).save(eq(user), any())`.

antipattern.symptom: Spring context fails to start: `No qualifying bean of type 'UtilityClass' available`.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent used `@MockBean` on a non-Spring-managed class (utility / value object).
antipattern.fix: Use plain `@Mock` (no Spring context) for non-bean classes. `@MockBean` is only for Spring-managed dependencies.

antipattern.symptom: Test module boots 90 s; each new test class blows the context cache; CI quota explodes.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent used `@SpringBootTest` for every test, including controller-only / repository-only cases.
antipattern.fix: Use the right slice annotation: `@WebMvcTest` / `@DataJpaTest` / plain Mockito. Reserve a single `@SpringBootTest` per module for cross-layer smoke.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass individually, flake when run in a specific order; later tests inherit a stale `LocalDateTime.now()` stub.
antipattern.root-cause: `mockStatic` was used without `try-with-resources`; the mock lingers.
antipattern.fix: Always wrap `MockedStatic` in `try (MockedStatic<T> ms = mockStatic(T.class)) { ... }`. Better: inject a `Clock` bean and avoid static mocking.

antipattern.symptom: `@SpringBootTest` repository test runs twice on CI; second run fails with `DuplicateKeyException` on seed data.
antipattern.root-cause: `@SpringBootTest` does not roll back per test by default; seed inserts persist.
antipattern.fix: Add `@Transactional` (rolls back per test) or `@Sql(executionPhase=AFTER_TEST_METHOD)` for explicit cleanup.
